epigraph.text: In him we live and move and have our being.
epigraph.quote: The chi-square, $t$, and $F$ distributions are the workhorses of statistical inference.

content.text:
Beyond the sample mean and proportion, other statistics have important 
sampling distributions.

content.title: Distribution of Sample Variance
content.content:
If $X_1, \ldots, X_n$ are i.i.d.\ $\Normal(\mu, \sigma^2)$, then:
\[
 \frac{(n-1)S^2}{\sigma^2} \sim \chi^2_{n-1}
\]
where $S^2 = \frac{1}{n-1}\sum_{i=1}^n (X_i - \xbar)^2$.
content.label: Theorem

content.text: When $\sigma$ is unknown and estimated by $S$:

content.title: $t$ Distribution for Sample Mean
content.content:
If $X_1, \ldots, X_n$ are i.i.d.\ $\Normal(\mu, \sigma^2)$:
\[
 T = \frac{\xbar - \mu}{S/\sqrt{n}} \sim t_{n-1}
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.text:
The $t$ distribution has heavier tails than the normal, accounting 
for the extra uncertainty from estimating $\sigma$.

content.title: $F$ Distribution
content.content:
If $U \sim \chi^2_m$ and $V \sim \chi^2_n$ are independent:
\[
 F = \frac{U/m}{V/n} \sim F_{m,n}
\]

content.text:
The $F$ distribution arises in comparing two variances:
\[
 F = \frac{S_1^2}{S_2^2} \sim F_{n_1-1, n_2-1}
\]
when sampling from two normal populations with equal variances.

marginNotes.text:
With God are wisdom and might; he has 
counsel and understanding.
